calendar.matters:
Petitioner And Respondents Joint Petition To Adopt Settlement Agreement And Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Shopoff for Lyndon Louie.
Gerard Richelson for Alvin Louie, Terry Louie and Steven Kay (Respondents).

Mr. Richelson stated that there is a confirmation and arbitration statute, and that this being a joint petition, it should be approved. The Court stated that it does not understand this to be an arbitration award. Mr. Shopoff stated that there was a negotiated resolution, and that part of the agreement is that it would become an award so that the parties could come to Court and get an order for a complete finality of the matter. Mr. Richelson stated that there may have been a drafting issue, made reference to the title of the petition, stated that this was intended to be a stipulated arbitration award and that he believes Judge Chiantelli would agree to that. The Court granted the Joint Petition to Adopt Settlement Agreement and Confirm Arbitration Award (subject to review of the order). Mr. Richelson provided a revised order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petitioner And Respondents Joint Petition To Adopt Settlement Agreement And Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Jul-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-01-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For Confirmation Of Prior Hems Distributions To Petitioner Lyndon K Louie And For Instruction As To Future Distributions To Petitioner Lyndon K Louie
Off Calendar May-01-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar May-01-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Millstein and Samantha Wu for Respondents
June Lee for Alvin Louie, Terry Louie, and Steven Kay (Respondents)
Thomas Huster and Jeffrey Shopoff for and with Lyndon Louie

Mr. Millstein stated that the house was going to be sold, they paid $224,000.00 in outstanding mortgage, asked the Court for guidance on monthly allowance, and reported that parties underwent mediation with Judge Kramer. Mr. Millstein also stated that parties will be going into arbitration, does not want to give more than $20,000.00 for monthly allowance, and that the mortgage has not been paid with the monthly allowance. Mr. Shopoff stated that he does not want the Court to make a determination today. The Court noted that the matter was not properly before the Court, hopes binding arbitration get done quickly to resolve the matter, and asked if there was urgency to affirm prior payments. Mr. Shopoff stated that there is urgency re: mortgage payment to avoid default. The Court stated that parties should be talking to each other. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc., and Petition for Confirmation of Prior HEMS Distributions, etc., are Off Calendar. The Motion for Summary for Adjudication set for hearing on July 12, 2018 is also Off Calendar at the request of parties.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T

Petition For Confirmation Of Prior Hems Distributions To Petitioner Lyndon K Louie And For Instruction As To Future Distributions To Petitioner Lyndon K Louie
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-12-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Continued To Apr-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster and Jeffrey Shopoff for and with Lyndon Louie.
David Millstein for Steven Kay, Terry Louie and Alvin Louie (Co-Trustees/Respondents).

Mr. Millstein stated that a forensic accounting is being prepared, that there will be mini-depositions after that and then mediation in March. Mr. Millstein also stated that it looks like they have collected the readily available records. Mr. Shopoff stated that the parties are cooperating, and that they want to move on mediation, but they need the accountants work first. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to April 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Retention Of Litigation Counsel And Accounting Expert
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Off Calendar

Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoenas Directed To (1) Capital One, (2) Wells Fargo, (3) Ubs, (4) American Express, (5) Jpmorgan Chase, (6) Merrill Lynch, (7) Bank Of The West, And (8) Ubs Bank Usa
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Off Calendar

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Aug-29-2017 Continued To Nov-08-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for Lyndon Louie (Beneficiary).
David Millstein for Trustees.

Mr. Millstein stated that they have not talked to each other, that he believes there will have to be some discovery, that having more information about numbers will help and that he would like to have the stipulation signed. Mr. Huster stated that he was told by the Examiner that the stipulation was needed, that he filed an amended petition and asked to have both matters heard on a 2:00 p.m. calendar. The Court stated that it will not move any petitions to an afternoon until it has seen all of the pleadings to determine whether an afternoon hearing is appropriate. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to November 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages the parties to talk to each other and figure out a sensible way through and try to settle issues of discovery.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Continued To Aug-29-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for and with Lyndon Louie (Petitioner).
David Millstein and June Lee for Steven Kay, Alvin Louie and Terry Louie (Respondents).

Mr. Huster stated that they have talked, that they haven't gotten any further on resolution, and that he has been trying to get mediation set, but there is no agreement. Ms. Lee stated that mediation is premature and that they are not open to mediation at this point. Mr. Millstein stated that there is litigation of the 2.2 million dollar agreement, that there is no forensic accounting, that petitions will be filed, that they are engaging in subpoenaing some records and suggested a 90-day status conference. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to August 29, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status, for the parties to get pleadings finalized, and for the parties to file whatever motions they think are appropriate so that by that date, they know how this matter is going to go.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Continued To Jun-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Huster for and with petitioner Lyndon Louie; June Lee and Alan Silver for respondents Stephen Kay, Alvin Louie and Terry Louie.

The Court noted that the accounting has now been filed, and asked if the parties had communicated about how to proceed from here. Mr. Huster replied that the parties have talked, but that a major issue remains regarding the disputed validity of a 2015 settlement agreement regarding an asset with a value of $3,000,000, which is a substantial portion of the estate. He said that the petitioner disputes the validity of the agreement, and he was unsure if the dispute should be resolved through an evidentiary hearing or a motion for summary judgment. The Court stated that he could not give the parties advice on how to proceed, as he did not have sufficient facts upon which to make a determination, and the question was not properly before his at this time. Ms. Lee stated that the respondents have filed tha accounting and made a settlement offer which is on the table. She added that Mr. Huster had requested an additional 10 days to consider the proposal, and that the extension of time has been granted. Mr. Silver stated that the Court should act now on the remaining relief requested in the petiton by denyinbg the request for removal. Mr. Huster requested that the Court grant a brief continuance to allow the parties to discuss the matter further and engage in some limited discovery. The matter was continued to June 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed that the parties, individually or jointly, submit by June 21 a statement of remaining issues to be addressed, unless an amended or new petition is filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Apr-18-2017 Continued To May-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for and with Lyndon Louie (Petitioner).
June Lee and Alan Silver for Steven Kay, Terry Louie and Alvin Louie (Respondents).

Ms. Lee stated that the accounting is almost completed, that there are still some pieces of information that are missing from both sides, that her Client is going through his records and checking with Banks, and that they need another two weeks to complete it. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to May 30, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court wants the accounting done and the Parties to be talking about how to resolve this.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Continued To Apr-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for and with Lyndon Louie (Beneficiary).

Mr. Huster stated they haven't actually mediated, that they have had a number of calls since the last hearing, that the accounting does not bring them closer to an understanding of the Trust, that he does not think the other parties abandoned their position and that he expected them to be here. Mr. Huster also stated that Lyndon has received from the Trust, spoke of Lyndon's financial needs, his mortgage and family medical issues, and suggested that $50,000.00 a month be paid to the Louie family going back to January of this year. Furthermore, Mr. Huster stated that they (the others) each took about $400,000.00 out of the Trust and that he believes they plan to provide an accounting within 30-days. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to April 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 one more time to see if information can be provided to get mediation on its way. The Court will sign an interim order requiring payment of $20,000.00 a month to Mr. Louie absent a showing why that is inappropriate prior to the next hearing. The Court will not make payment retroactive. Payment begins the month of March going forward. Mr. Huster is to prepare a form of order and provide it to the other side for approval as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Continued To Mar-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for Lyndon Louie (Petitioner).
June Lee and Alan Silver for Stephen Kay, Alvin Louie and Terry Louie (Respondents).

The Parties reported that there is still a dispute with regard to the time frame of the accounting (from 2011 or 2014), the issue of the promissory note, outstanding information on the bank accounts and the Trustees not providing for Lyndon's needs. Mr. Huster stated that Lyndon has agreed to pay the note on the condition that he be given a $500,000.00 line of credit, that payments be deferred for five years and that he receive a $15,000.00 monthly allotment. Mr. Huster also stated that he would be happy to provide an accounting for the $1,000,000.00 Bank of The West account. The accountings by the Trustees are to cover the entire corpus of the Trust whether it is by getting all of the materials to the Trustees, or it being done in pieces by both the Trustees and the Petitioner. Said accounting is to done no later than March 7, 2017. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc. is continued to March 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that prior to that the date, the parties have met and conferred on resolving the matter or how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

Petition For 1) Removal Of Trustees 2) Appointment Of Independent Successor Trustee 3) Instruction T
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Continued To Jan-24-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Huster for and with Lyndon Louie. Alan Silver & June Lee for (respondents) Terry Louie, Alvin Louie & Steven Kay.

The Court stated that there is a supplement with changes, that the relief was not adequately noticed and will need to be continued and asked if there will be opposition. Mr. Huster replied that there will be. Ms. Lee stated that they e-filed their opposition yesterday afternoon and that they will provide courtesy copies. Mr. Huster stated that he will be filing an ex parte petition. The Petition for Removal of Trustees, etc., was continued to January 24, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proper notice and to give the Court time to look at the opposition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
